title: calendula suffruticosa subsp. algarbiensis
intro: Calendula suffruticosa subsp. algarbiensis, also known as Algarve marsh marigold, is a subspecies of the Calendula suffruticosa plant, a type of flowering plant in the daisy family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help soothe and heal skin irritations and wounds. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system, making it a popular ingredient in natural skincare products and herbal remedies. Calendula suffruticosa subsp. algarbiensis can be used in various herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
